name: 理查德·瓦格纳
brief: 瓦格纳出生于莱比锡，是音乐史上最具争议性的天才之一。他不仅是作曲家，还是剧作家、指挥家、散文家和剧院建筑师。他的一生充满戏剧性——政治流亡、债务缠身、与李斯特女儿柯西玛的婚外情、巴伐利亚国王路德维希二世的狂热赞助。他在拜罗伊特建造了专门上演自己作品的节日剧院（Festspielhaus），这座剧院至今仍在运营。
briefEn: Wagner was born in Leipzig and stands as one of the most controversial geniuses in the history of music. He was not only a composer but also a dramatist, conductor, essayist, and theater architect. His life was filled with drama — political exile, crushing debts, an affair with Liszt's daughter Cosima, and the fanatical patronage of King Ludwig II of Bavaria. He built a festival theater (Festspielhaus) in Bayreuth dedicated exclusively to his own works, and it continues to operate to this day.
contribution: 瓦格纳创造了「总体艺术品」（Gesamtkunstwerk）的理念——将音乐、戏剧、诗歌、视觉艺术融为一个不可分割的有机整体。他发明了「主导动机」（Leitmotiv）体系——用特定的音乐片段代表人物、事物、情感和概念，通过动机的变形、叠加和交织来推动戏剧发展。他将调性和声推向了极限，《特里斯坦与伊索尔德》的开场和弦被视为调性瓦解的开端。
contributionEn: Wagner conceived the idea of the Gesamtkunstwerk — the total work of art — fusing music, drama, poetry, and visual art into a single, inseparable organic whole. He developed the leitmotif system, assigning specific musical fragments to characters, objects, emotions, and ideas, and driving the drama forward through their transformation, superimposition, and interweaving. He pushed tonal harmony to its breaking point; the opening chord of Tristan und Isolde is widely regarded as the moment when traditional tonality began to dissolve.
style: 瓦格纳的音乐以「无终旋律」（Unendliche Melodie）为特征——音乐不再被分割成独立的咏叹调和宣叙调，而是形成一条无穷无尽的旋律长河。他的管弦乐写作极其丰厚，创造了前所未有的音响世界。他大幅扩大了乐队编制（要求大号中的「瓦格纳大号」就是他的发明），创造了一种近乎催眠般的沉浸式音乐体验。
styleEn: Wagner's music is defined by unendliche Melodie — endless melody — in which the music is no longer divided into discrete arias and recitatives but flows as one infinite melodic river. His orchestral writing is extraordinarily rich, conjuring sonic worlds never heard before. He greatly expanded the size of the orchestra (the Wagner tuba was his own invention) and created an almost hypnotic, immersive musical experience.

famousScores.name: 《尼伯龙根的指环》
famousScores.desc: 四联剧（莱茵的黄金、女武神、齐格弗里德、众神的黄昏），约15小时
famousScores.descEn: A tetralogy (Das Rheingold, Die Walküre, Siegfried, Götterdämmerung) spanning approximately 15 hours
famousScores.detail:
《尼伯龙根的指环》是瓦格纳最宏大的创作，从最初构想到最终完成历时26年（1848—1874年）。这个四联剧改编自北欧神话和德国尼伯龙根史诗，以阿尔贝里希的黄金魔咒开启，以众神殿堂瓦尔哈拉的大火告终——整个神族与英雄的世界在腐败与权力欲的腐蚀下走向毁灭。瓦格纳不仅写了音乐，还独立完成了全部的剧诗，历时多年，工程之浩大令人叹为观止。

《指环》的首演在拜罗伊特节日剧院于1876年举行，国王路德维希二世、皇帝威廉一世、巴西皇帝佩德罗二世以及来自欧洲各地的音乐家（包括李斯特、勃拉姆斯、圣-桑斯等）悉数出席。首演结束后，瓦格纳走到台前，说了一句成为音乐史名言的话：「我已经创造了一种世界艺术，今晚你们见到了它的开始。」然而他随后发现剧场严重亏损，令他陷入了严重的财务危机。

《指环》对整个西方文化的影响无法用言语完全衡量。其「腐败的权力毁灭世界」的主题被视为对现代资本主义和政治权力的深刻预言；托尔金的《指环王》在人物和神话体系上与瓦格纳的《指环》之间存在可探讨的影响关系（尽管托尔金本人否认）。好莱坞电影配乐中的主导动机体系几乎可以直接回溯至《指环》的创作原则。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Ring cycle was Wagner's supreme creation, twenty-six years in the making (1848–1874). The tetralogy is drawn from Norse mythology and the German Nibelungenlied epic, opening with Alberich's golden curse and ending with the conflagration of Valhalla — an entire world of gods and heroes consumed by the corruption born of greed and the lust for power. Wagner wrote not only the music but all four librettos himself over many years, an achievement of staggering scale.

The premiere was held at the Bayreuth Festspielhaus in 1876, attended by King Ludwig II, Kaiser Wilhelm I, Emperor Pedro II of Brazil, and musicians from across Europe including Liszt, Brahms, and Saint-Saëns. After the final performance, Wagner stepped forward and made a statement that became one of music's most celebrated utterances: 'I have created a world artwork; tonight you have seen its beginning.' He then discovered the theater had run a severe deficit, plunging him into financial crisis.

The Ring's influence on Western culture is impossible to fully measure. Its theme of 'corrupted power destroying the world' is widely read as a prophetic critique of modern capitalism and political authority. Tolkien's The Lord of the Rings shares enough mythological and character parallels with the Ring to invite comparisons (though Tolkien denied any debt). Hollywood film scoring's leitmotif system can be traced almost directly back to the Ring's compositional principles.

famousScores.name: 《特里斯坦与伊索尔德》
famousScores.desc: 音乐史上最革命性的作品之一，开场和弦改变了和声的历史
famousScores.descEn: One of the most revolutionary works in music history; its opening chord changed the course of harmony
famousScores.detail:
《特里斯坦与伊索尔德》创作于1857至1859年间，是瓦格纳与已婚女子玛蒂尔德·魏森东克（其赞助人的妻子）的激情恋情的直接产物。这段无法实现的爱情成为整部歌剧的精神内核：爱情因无法在世俗世界得到满足而只能在死亡中得到完成——这一主题被称为「特里斯坦的欲望哲学」，深受叔本华哲学的影响。整部歌剧近五小时，几乎没有传统意义上的「动作」，只有爱欲、渴望和死亡的持续累积。

著名的「特里斯坦和弦」（第一个升F-B-升D-升G和弦）之所以在音乐史上具有如此特殊的地位，是因为它巧妙地回避了解决，使调性处于永久的不稳定状态。从1865年首演至今，已有整整一个世纪的音乐学家争论这个和弦究竟属于哪个调性分析范畴。这个和弦的「不可解决性」直接预示了无调性音乐的到来，被认为是西方调性音乐体系走向终结的起点。

《特里斯坦与伊索尔德》对后世的影响，在德彪西的《佩利亚斯与梅丽桑德》、理查·施特劳斯的《玫瑰骑士》和勋伯格的早期作品中最为明显。尼采在早期是瓦格纳的狂热崇拜者，他曾说《特里斯坦》是「人类写出的最美作品」；后来他与瓦格纳决裂，却仍无法否认这部歌剧的音乐魔力。
famousScores.detailEn:
Tristan und Isolde was composed in 1857–59, the direct product of Wagner's passionate affair with Mathilde Wesendonck, the wife of his patron. This unrealizable love became the spiritual core of the opera: love that cannot be fulfilled in the earthly world can only be consummated in death — a philosophy deeply influenced by Schopenhauer, known as 'Tristan's philosophy of desire.' The opera runs nearly five hours and contains almost no conventional 'action' — only the sustained accumulation of longing, erotic passion, and death.

The famous 'Tristan chord' (the opening F-sharp–B–D-sharp–G-sharp sonority) holds its special place in music history because it so artfully withholds resolution, keeping tonality in a state of permanent instability. An entire century of music theorists, from 1865 to the present, have debated which tonal category this chord belongs to. Its 'irresolvability' directly anticipated atonal music and is widely regarded as the moment when the Western tonal system began its long process of dissolution.

Tristan's influence on subsequent music is most visible in Debussy's Pelléas et Mélisande, Richard Strauss's Der Rosenkavalier, and Schoenberg's early works. Nietzsche, an early fervent Wagnerian, called Tristan 'the most beautiful thing human beings have ever written'; even after his bitter break with Wagner, he could not deny the opera's musical sorcery.

famousScores.name: 《纽伦堡的名歌手》
famousScores.desc: 唯一的喜歌剧，对艺术与传统关系的深刻思考
famousScores.descEn: His only comic opera — a profound meditation on the relationship between art and tradition
famousScores.detail:
《纽伦堡的名歌手》是瓦格纳唯一的喜歌剧，也是他所有歌剧中篇幅最长的一部（约五至六小时）。创作于1861至1867年间，以16世纪纽伦堡的「名歌手」（匠人歌唱行会）为背景，讲述了年轻骑士瓦尔特如何在鞋匠兼诗人汉斯·萨克斯的指引下，以充满创新精神的歌唱赢得评委和爱人的故事。故事的核心是艺术创新（瓦尔特的新歌）与传统规则（名歌手行会的条规）之间的冲突与和解。

序曲是整部歌剧中最广为人知的段落，以辉煌的C大调将多个主导动机同时对位叠加，被认为是西方对位音乐的杰作之一。汉斯·萨克斯的人物形象是瓦格纳歌剧中最复杂、最人性化的一个——一个甘愿放弃个人幸福以成就年轻人的理想老人，其独白「疯狂！疯狂！无处不在的疯狂！」被认为是对整个人类自我欺骗的深刻讽喻。

这部歌剧在历史上因被纳粹德国（尤其是希特勒）作为德意志文化优越性的象征而声誉受损，其终幕中汉斯·萨克斯高歌「保留神圣的德意志艺术！」的段落被纳粹仪式大量使用。这段历史至今仍是拜罗伊特演出这部歌剧时无法回避的文化包袱，也是讨论「伟大艺术与邪恶政治的纠缠」这一问题时最经常被引用的案例之一。
famousScores.detailEn:
Die Meistersinger von Nürnberg is Wagner's only comic opera and the longest of all his works (approximately five to six hours). Composed 1861–67, it is set in sixteenth-century Nuremberg among the Meistersingers — a guild of artisan singers — and tells how the young knight Walther, guided by the cobbler-poet Hans Sachs, wins both the jury's prize and his beloved through singing full of creative daring. Its central subject is the conflict and reconciliation between artistic innovation (Walther's new song) and traditional rules (the guild's regulations).

The overture is the work's best-known passage, a brilliant C major tour de force simultaneously weaving together multiple leitmotifs in counterpoint, widely considered one of the masterpieces of Western contrapuntal writing. Hans Sachs is the most complex and fully human character in all of Wagner's operas — an idealized elder who sacrifices personal happiness to serve the young — and his monologue 'Wahn! Wahn! Überall Wahn!' ('Madness, madness, everywhere madness!') is a profound meditation on human self-delusion.

The opera's reputation has been shadowed by its adoption by Nazi Germany — especially Hitler — as a symbol of German cultural supremacy; the finale, in which Sachs sings 'Honour your German masters!', was extensively used in Nazi ceremonies. This history remains an unavoidable cultural burden at every Bayreuth performance and is one of the most frequently cited cases in discussions of the entanglement of great art and evil politics.

famousScores.name: 《帕西法尔》
famousScores.desc: 晚年最后的「舞台神圣节日剧」，充满宗教神秘感
famousScores.descEn: His final 'sacred stage festival play,' steeped in religious mysticism
famousScores.detail:
《帕西法尔》是瓦格纳的最后一部歌剧，完成于1882年，在拜罗伊特节日剧院首演。这部歌剧基于中世纪亚瑟王传说的圣杯故事，探讨了「通过同情获得救赎」的宗教哲学主题，以一个简单、纯洁的愚者（帕西法尔）治愈了因罪孽而饱受折磨的国王安弗尔塔斯为主要情节。叔本华哲学与基督教神学的融合使这部作品带有极深的神秘主义色彩。

瓦格纳指定《帕西法尔》只能在拜罗伊特演出——他将其称为「舞台神圣节日剧」（Bühnenweihfestspiel），认为只有这座专为他设计的剧院才能正确呈现这部作品的神圣氛围。这个专演权维持了三十年，直到1913年才随版权到期而告终。1903年，纽约大都会歌剧院无视这个专演权强行上演了《帕西法尔》，引发了瓦格纳家族的强烈抗议，成为音乐史上著名的版权纠纷。

《帕西法尔》的音乐是瓦格纳最富有灵性色彩的创作，第一幕前奏曲中「信仰动机」的庄严与圣洁，至今仍被认为是宗教音乐中最令人震撼的开篇之一。尼采后来在《尼采反对瓦格纳》中将这部作品作为瓦格纳「向基督教投降」的证据进行批判；但也有许多人认为，这恰恰是瓦格纳一生中最诚实的精神自白。
famousScores.detailEn:
Parsifal was Wagner's final opera, completed in 1882 and premiered at the Bayreuth Festspielhaus. Based on the medieval Arthurian Grail legend, it explores the religious-philosophical theme of 'redemption through compassion,' centering on a pure and simple-minded fool (Parsifal) who heals the tortured king Amfortas, who is suffering for his sins. The fusion of Schopenhauer's philosophy with Christian theology gives the work a profoundly mystical character.

Wagner stipulated that Parsifal could be performed only at Bayreuth — he called it a Bühnenweihfestspiel ('sacred stage festival play'), believing only his specially designed theater could rightly embody its spiritual atmosphere. This exclusive performance right lasted thirty years before expiring in 1913. In 1903 the Metropolitan Opera in New York defied the restriction and staged Parsifal anyway, provoking furious protests from the Wagner family and producing one of the most famous copyright disputes in music history.

Parsifal's music is Wagner's most spiritually luminous creation; the solemn and radiant 'Faith motif' in the Act I Prelude is still considered one of the most awe-inspiring openings in all religious music. Nietzsche later attacked the work in Nietzsche contra Wagner as evidence of Wagner's 'surrender to Christianity'; others regard it as the most honest spiritual confession of Wagner's life.

famousScores.name: 《女武神的骑行》
famousScores.desc: 《指环》第二部的管弦乐名段，影视配乐中引用最多的古典音乐
famousScores.descEn: The iconic orchestral passage from the second Ring opera — the most frequently quoted classical music in film
famousScores.detail:
《女武神的骑行》是《尼伯龙根的指环》第二部《女武神》第三幕的开场音乐，描绘八位女武神（战场上拣选死亡英雄灵魂的女神）骑马驾云在风暴中驰骋集结的壮烈场面。这段音乐以管乐的节奏性分解和弦为骑行节奏，弦乐以强劲的旋律勾勒出女武神们的战吼——是整个交响乐文献中最具物理冲击力的段落之一。

这段音乐在电影史上最著名的使用是弗朗西斯·科波拉1979年电影《现代启示录》中越战直升机进攻场面的配乐，那个将瓦格纳的帝国主义象征音乐与越战的暴力画面并置的选择，成为了影史上最具争议性的音乐使用之一，也使这段音乐成为「以文明的暴力掩盖野蛮侵略」的有力隐喻。此后这段音乐在通俗文化中的引用次数多到难以统计。

《女武神的骑行》是瓦格纳最广为人知的单段音乐，在「最著名古典音乐片段」的各类排名中长期位居前列。它对管弦乐「壮丽进行曲」传统的影响是决定性的，从约翰·菲利普·苏萨的进行曲到好莱坞战争电影配乐，这段音乐所建立的「战斗音乐」模式成为了后世所有同类创作的隐性标准。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Ride of the Valkyries opens Act III of Die Walküre, the second opera of the Ring cycle, depicting eight Valkyrie warrior-goddesses — who choose the fallen heroes on battlefields — riding on horseback through the storm to their mountain gathering place. The brass percussion rhythm evokes the gallop, while the strings carve out the Valkyries' battle cry — one of the most physically thrilling passages in the entire orchestral literature.

The music's most celebrated cinematic use was Francis Ford Coppola's decision to score a helicopter assault sequence in his 1979 film Apocalypse Now with this passage. Juxtaposing Wagner's imperialist symbol music with images of Vietnam War violence, the choice became one of the most debated musical uses in film history and gave the music a new layer of meaning as a powerful metaphor for 'civilized brutality masking barbaric aggression.' Its citation count in popular culture thereafter became incalculable.

The Ride of the Valkyries is Wagner's most universally recognized single passage, consistently ranking near the top of 'most famous classical music excerpts' lists worldwide. Its influence on the orchestral 'grand march' tradition was decisive — from John Philip Sousa's marches to Hollywood war scoring, the 'battle music' model this passage established became the implicit standard for all similar writing that followed.

famousScores.name: 《漂泊的荷兰人》
famousScores.desc: 瓦格纳第一部成熟歌剧，海上幽灵传说与救赎主题的震撼呈现
famousScores.descEn: Wagner's first mature opera — a thundering sea-storm and the ghost ship legend as music drama
famousScores.detail:
《漂泊的荷兰人》创作于1840至1841年，是瓦格纳第一部真正属于自己风格的歌剧，与他早期的意大利风格模仿作品截然不同。创作灵感来自他1839年从里加乘船赴巴黎途中在波罗的海遭遇的一场近乎致命的海上风暴——那三天三夜与死神擦肩的经历使他对「漂泊的荷兰人」传说（被诅咒的幽灵船长永远漂泊海上，每七年才能上岸一次寻找能以死亡之爱救赎他的女子）产生了切身的情感共鸣。

序曲是音乐史上最具画面感的「暴风雨」描绘之一：管弦乐以惊涛骇浪般的音响开篇，「荷兰人动机」如幽灵般在其间闪现——这段序曲已成为西方管弦乐曲目中描绘海洋与命运的经典参照。女主角辛塔对荷兰人的「忠诚至死」的爱情主题是瓦格纳此后众多「救赎」作品的最早原型，《指环》《特里斯坦》《帕西法尔》中的救赎主题都可以追溯到辛塔这个人物身上。

《漂泊的荷兰人》在瓦格纳歌剧的发展脉络中具有转折性意义：它标志着他从传统的「号码歌剧」（分段的咏叹调和重唱）向「无终旋律」的「乐剧」（Music Drama）的过渡。尽管这部作品在形式上仍有一些早期歌剧的痕迹，但其戏剧深度和音乐统一性已经远超同时代的歌剧，预示了瓦格纳随后几十年的革命性发展。
famousScores.detailEn:
Der fliegende Holländer was composed in 1840–41 and is Wagner's first opera genuinely in his own style, entirely unlike his earlier Italian-influenced works. The inspiration came from a near-fatal storm Wagner encountered in the Baltic Sea in 1839, while sailing from Riga to Paris — three terrifying days and nights in the grip of the tempest that gave him visceral personal empathy with the legend of the Flying Dutchman (a cursed ghost captain doomed to roam the seas forever, allowed ashore once every seven years to seek a woman whose love unto death could redeem him).

The overture is one of music history's most vividly pictorial 'storm' descriptions: the orchestra opens with wave-crash force as the 'Dutchman motif' flickers through the tumult like a ghost — this overture has become the classic Western orchestral reference point for depicting the sea and fate. The heroine Senta's 'redemption through faithful love unto death' is the earliest prototype of the redemption theme that runs through all of Wagner's subsequent works; the redemption arcs of the Ring, Tristan, and Parsifal can all be traced back to Senta's character.

The Flying Dutchman marks a turning point in the development of Wagnerian opera: it signals his transition from the traditional 'number opera' (discrete arias and ensembles) toward the continuous 'endless melody' of Music Drama. Though the work still bears some formal traces of early opera, its dramatic depth and musical unity far surpass anything by his contemporaries, foreshadowing Wagner's revolutionary development over the following decades.

famousScores.name: 《唐豪瑟》
famousScores.desc: 肉体欢愉与精神救赎的永恒冲突，序曲是瓦格纳最常被单独演奏的管弦乐作品
famousScores.descEn: The eternal conflict between carnal pleasure and spiritual redemption — the overture is Wagner's most popular standalone orchestral piece
famousScores.detail:
《唐豪瑟》创作于1843至1845年，首演于1845年德累斯顿，由瓦格纳本人指挥。这部歌剧的核心戏剧冲突是维纳斯山（肉体欢愉的异教殿堂）与瓦尔特堡（基督教精神荣耀的世界）之间的永恒拉锯——骑士唐豪瑟夹在维纳斯的肉体诱惑和伊丽莎白纯洁之爱的精神召唤之间，无法取舍，最终走向毁灭与救赎。这个主题深刻地反映了瓦格纳本人内心的艺术家身份与世俗欲望之间的永恒矛盾。

1861年，瓦格纳应拿破仑三世之邀将《唐豪瑟》带到巴黎歌剧院演出——这是他一生中最惨烈的失败之一。巴黎剧场的权贵们（「骑士团」成员）因为瓦格纳拒绝在第二幕加入传统芭蕾（按照巴黎传统，第二幕必须有芭蕾以满足晚场到达的贵族观众）而组织起系统性的嘲笑和喝倒彩，连续三场演出都被破坏，最终不得不撤演。这次「巴黎惨败」成为音乐史上最著名的首演丑闻之一。

《唐豪瑟》序曲是音乐会中单独演奏频率最高的瓦格纳管弦乐作品，它将「维纳斯堡音乐」（欲望与沉沦）与「朝圣队伍」（救赎与光明）两种对立音乐材料的戏剧性对比浓缩在二十分钟内，是理解瓦格纳「总体艺术品」理念的最好入门。这部歌剧对布鲁克纳、沃尔夫和理查·施特劳斯的影响最为直接深远。
famousScores.detailEn:
Tannhäuser was composed in 1843–45 and premiered in Dresden in 1845, conducted by Wagner himself. The opera's dramatic core is the eternal tug-of-war between the Venusberg (a pagan temple of carnal pleasure) and the Wartburg (the world of Christian spiritual glory) — the knight Tannhäuser, torn between Venus's bodily temptation and the pure spiritual love of Elisabeth, cannot choose, and is driven toward destruction and redemption. The theme deeply mirrors Wagner's own lifelong conflict between his artistic identity and worldly desire.

In 1861, Napoleon III invited Wagner to bring Tannhäuser to the Paris Opéra — it became one of the most catastrophic defeats of his career. The Parisian Jockey Club, furious that Wagner refused to insert a traditional ballet in Act II (required by Parisian convention to accommodate aristocrats who arrived late), organized systematic booing and whistling across three consecutive performances until the production was withdrawn. The 'Paris catastrophe' became one of the most infamous premiere disasters in music history.

The Tannhäuser Overture is the most frequently performed standalone Wagner orchestral work in concert, compressing into twenty minutes the dramatic confrontation between 'Venusberg music' (desire and descent) and the 'Pilgrims' Chorus' (redemption and light) — an ideal introduction to Wagner's Gesamtkunstwerk concept. Tannhäuser's direct influence on Bruckner, Wolf, and Richard Strauss was especially profound.

famousScores.name: 《罗恩格林》
famousScores.desc: 神圣骑士的悲剧，序曲的天国光芒与「婚礼进行曲」家喻户晓
famousScores.descEn: The tragedy of the divine knight; the heavenly Prelude and the 'Bridal Chorus' are known the world over
famousScores.detail:
《罗恩格林》创作于1845至1848年，首演于1850年的魏玛，由李斯特亲自指挥——此时瓦格纳因参与1848年德累斯顿革命而流亡在外，无法亲历自己这部作品的首演。这部歌剧以中世纪圣杯骑士罗恩格林（帕西法尔之子）的传说为基础，他受命保卫遭诬陷的艾尔莎，条件是她永远不能询问他的名字和来历；当爱尔莎终于在怀疑的驱使下提出了那个禁忌的问题，罗恩格林被迫离去，爱情走向了悲剧。

《罗恩格林》中最广为人知的段落是第三幕的「婚礼进行曲」（Bridal Chorus，即「忠诚与诚实，领来这对新人」），这段音乐从1858年英国维多利亚公主婚礼首次使用后，迅速成为西方婚礼仪式中最常用的音乐之一，至今仍是全球新人在教堂走向圣坛时最标志性的旋律。第一幕前奏曲以极弱的弦乐高音开篇，描绘圣杯从天而降的神圣景象，被认为是整个浪漫主义时期最精致的「天国音响」描绘。

《罗恩格林》对欧洲文化的影响超出了音乐范畴。巴伐利亚国王路德维希二世对这部歌剧几近病态的痴迷，使他决定建造「新天鹅堡」（Neuschwanstein）——那座著名的童话城堡的内部装饰大量取材于《罗恩格林》的场景。这座城堡后来成为迪士尼「睡美人城堡」的原型，将《罗恩格林》的影响以最间接的方式延伸到了现代大众文化。
famousScores.detailEn:
Lohengrin was composed in 1845–48 and premiered in Weimar in 1850, conducted by Liszt — Wagner was in exile for his role in the Dresden Revolution of 1848 and could not attend the premiere of his own opera. Based on the legend of the Grail knight Lohengrin (son of Parsifal), it tells how he is sent to defend the falsely accused Elsa on condition that she never ask his name or origin; when doubt finally drives Elsa to ask the forbidden question, Lohengrin is compelled to leave, and their love ends in tragedy.

The most universally known passage from Lohengrin is the Act III Bridal Chorus ('Treulich geführt,' popularly known as 'Here Comes the Bride'): since its first use at the wedding of Princess Victoria of Great Britain in 1858, it rapidly became the most widely used music in Western wedding ceremonies and remains, to this day, the world's most iconic processional melody. The Act I Prelude, opening with barely audible high strings, depicts the Holy Grail descending from heaven and is considered the most refined 'celestial sound painting' in all of Romantic music.

Lohengrin's cultural influence extended far beyond music. King Ludwig II of Bavaria's near-pathological obsession with the opera drove his decision to build Neuschwanstein — the famous fairy-tale castle whose interior decorations are largely drawn from Lohengrin's scenes. That castle became the model for Disney's Sleeping Beauty Castle, extending Lohengrin's influence into modern popular culture by the most indirect of routes.

famousScores.name: 《齐格弗里德牧歌》
famousScores.desc: 为妻子柯西玛生日所作的私密室内曲，温柔是瓦格纳鲜为人知的另一面
famousScores.descEn: A private serenade written for his wife Cosima's birthday — the tender, intimate side of Wagner rarely seen
famousScores.detail:
《齐格弗里德牧歌》创作于1870年，是瓦格纳为妻子柯西玛（李斯特之女）庆祝圣诞与33岁生日所作的秘密礼物。1870年12月25日清晨，柯西玛从梦中醒来，听到屋外传来一阵轻柔的音乐——这是瓦格纳事先安排了一支小型室内乐队悄悄爬上他们特里布申别墅的楼梯演奏的。柯西玛在日记中写道：「我的泪水涌出，眼前的一切都消失在泪光中。」这是音乐史上最动人的私人赠礼时刻之一。

作品以小型室内乐队（13件乐器）演奏，融合了《指环》中的「齐格弗里德动机」和一首瓦格纳早年为未来孩子创作的摇篮曲（这时柯西玛刚刚在1869年生下了他们的儿子齐格弗里德）。整部作品充满了一种极其罕见的瓦格纳情感——温柔、宁静、私密，与他那些庞大的歌剧完全不同，如同一个不为人知的私人空间。他们的儿子齐格弗里德就是这首音乐所描绘的新生命。

《齐格弗里德牧歌》揭示了瓦格纳性格中一个通常被宏大歌剧所遮蔽的侧面：他对家庭生活和私人情感也有极深的感受能力。这部作品对后世的「家庭室内音乐」传统有深远影响，理查·施特劳斯的《家庭交响曲》和马勒的《田园交响曲》中那些温柔段落，都可以在《齐格弗里德牧歌》中找到遥远的回响。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Siegfried Idyll was composed in 1870 as a secret birthday and Christmas gift for his wife Cosima (Liszt's daughter). On the morning of December 25, 1870, Cosima awoke to the sound of soft music outside her bedroom — Wagner had secretly arranged for a small chamber ensemble to climb the stairs of their villa at Tribschen and play beneath her window. Cosima wrote in her diary: 'I was in tears and could not find my way through them.' It is one of the most moving private gift moments in all music history.

The work is scored for a small chamber ensemble of thirteen instruments and blends the 'Siegfried motif' from the Ring with a lullaby Wagner had written for a future child — Cosima had given birth to their son Siegfried in 1869. The entire piece radiates an atmosphere of extraordinary rarity in Wagner: tenderness, serenity, and intimacy, completely unlike his gigantic operas — a private world no one else was meant to enter. The newborn son Siegfried is the living spirit the music describes.

The Siegfried Idyll reveals a side of Wagner's character usually hidden behind the monumental operas: his deep capacity for private emotion and domestic love. This work exerted a lasting influence on the chamber music of the home tradition; the tender passages of Richard Strauss's Symphonia Domestica and Mahler's pastoral moments both carry distant echoes of the Siegfried Idyll.

famousScores.name: 《魏森东克歌曲集》
famousScores.desc: 五首为女高音与钢琴所作的艺术歌曲，《特里斯坦》前奏曲的姊妹篇
famousScores.descEn: Five songs for soprano and piano — intimate companions to the Tristan prelude, born of the same forbidden love
famousScores.detail:
《魏森东克歌曲集》创作于1857至1858年，歌词由瓦格纳的赞助人之妻、他的秘密恋人玛蒂尔德·魏森东克所写。这五首歌曲（《天使》《驻足》《温室里》《悲悼》《梦》）中的最后两首，瓦格纳本人将其标注为「《特里斯坦与伊索尔德》的习作」——即这两首歌曲与《特里斯坦》的前奏曲和第二幕使用了相同的和声材料，是同一时期的姊妹作品。

这套歌曲是一段无法实现的爱情最直接的音乐表达：玛蒂尔德有夫之妇，瓦格纳是她丈夫的客人，两人的感情必须隐藏在社交礼仪的面纱之下。据说最后一首《梦》（Träume）的旋律，后来被移植进了《特里斯坦》第二幕的爱情二重唱——那是整部歌剧中两个爱人最接近完全结合的时刻。"梦中之梦"的层叠结构是这段秘密情感最精妙的音乐隐喻。

《魏森东克歌曲集》在艺术歌曲传统中占据独特地位，是瓦格纳几乎唯一广泛演唱的声乐室内乐作品。它的和声语言是《特里斯坦》音乐语言的缩影版，对于希望理解「特里斯坦和声」但又不想坐五小时歌剧的听众而言，这套歌曲是最完美的入门。理查·施特劳斯后来为这套歌曲写了管弦乐配器版，使其成为音乐会女高音的重要曲目。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Wesendonck Lieder were composed in 1857–58 to poems by Mathilde Wesendonck, his patron's wife and secret beloved. Of the five songs (Angel, Stop!, In the Greenhouse, Sorrows, Dreams), Wagner himself marked the last two as 'studies for Tristan und Isolde' — they share harmonic material with the Tristan Prelude and Act II, having been written simultaneously as sister works.

The cycle is the most direct musical expression of an unrealizable love: Mathilde was a married woman and Wagner was her husband's guest; their feelings had to remain hidden beneath the veil of social propriety. The melody of the final song, Dreams (Träume), is said to have been transplanted directly into the love duet of Tristan Act II — the moment in the opera when the two lovers come closest to complete union. The layered dream-within-dream structure is the most artful musical metaphor for this hidden passion.

The Wesendonck Lieder occupy a unique place in the art song tradition, representing almost the only vocal chamber music by Wagner in wide concert use. Their harmonic language is a concentrated version of Tristan's musical idiom, making them the ideal introduction for those who wish to understand 'Tristan harmony' without sitting through five hours of opera. Richard Strauss later orchestrated the songs, establishing them as an important repertoire item for the concert soprano.

influence: 瓦格纳的影响远超音乐领域。他的和声创新直接催生了勋伯格的无调性音乐；他的主导动机技法被好莱坞电影配乐全面继承（约翰·威廉斯、霍华德·肖等）；他的「总体艺术品」理念影响了现代多媒体艺术。尼采、波德莱尔、托马斯·曼等文学家和哲学家都深受其影响。尽管因其反犹主义言论和纳粹对其作品的挪用而备受争议，瓦格纳在音乐史上的革命性地位无可动摇。
influenceEn: Wagner's influence extends far beyond music. His harmonic innovations led directly to Schoenberg's atonality; his leitmotif technique was adopted wholesale by Hollywood film composers (John Williams, Howard Shore, and others); and his Gesamtkunstwerk ideal shaped modern multimedia art. Philosophers and writers including Nietzsche, Baudelaire, and Thomas Mann were profoundly affected by his work. Though deeply controversial for his antisemitic writings and the Nazi appropriation of his music, Wagner's revolutionary standing in the history of music remains unshakeable.
